steps.title: Recognize the condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: When a system of linear equations has infinitely many solutions, the two equations represent the same line—one is a scalar multiple of the other.

steps.title: Find the scale factor by comparing coefficients
steps.content:
Look at the y-coefficients: the first equation has 9, the second has 18. The scale factor is:
$$k = \frac{18}{9} = 2$$

The second equation should be exactly 2 times the first.

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to find r
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by 2:
$$2(3x + 9y) = 2(10)$$
$$6x + 18y = 20$$

This must equal the second equation $rx + 18y = 20$. Comparing the x-coefficients: $r = 6$.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
